name: flowio
tagline: Python library skill for parsing/writing Flow Cytometry Standard (FCS) files into NumPy arrays, CSV, and back.
verdict: Clean, low-risk documentation skill. It teaches local file I/O with the flowio PyPI package only — no network calls, no credential access, no shell-piped remote code, no obfuscation. Only real nits: it runs ~606 lines (over the ~500-line budget) and never states an explicit 'do not use for X' boundary beyond pointing to FlowKit for advanced analysis.

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do not use when' section; the 'Related Tools' note (line 26) pointing to FlowKit for compensation/gating implies a boundary but never states it as a negative trigger.
Content is fairly verbose with repeated/overlapping examples across 'Core Workflows', 'Common Use Cases', and 'Integration Notes' sections, costing more context than strictly needed.
File is 606 lines, roughly 20% over the ~500-line budget guideline, mostly due to repetitive example sections.
